sections.heading: Architecture Overview
sections.content:
The detail panel is implemented in `detail.js` (~500 lines). It renders as a slide-out drawer on the right side of the screen when a node is selected, organized into **5 tabs**: Overview, Docs, API, Relationships, History.

**State management:**
- `Detail._activeTab` — persists across `show()` calls within the session (tab selection survives node switches)
- `Detail._cache` — per-node lazy-fetch cache (reset on each `show()` call), stores edges data fetched on Relationships tab activation
- `Detail._nodeData`, `_historyData`, `_stalenessStatus`, `_dflowStepsData`, `_testsData`, `_stalenessCause` — stored from `show()` args, accessed by tab renderers

**Data fetched per selection (parallel in App.selectNode):**
1. `GET /api/nodes/{id}` — full node object (all fields)
2. `GET /api/nodes/{id}/history` — change log entries
3. `GET /api/nodes/{id}/tests` — test results
4. `GET /api/nodes/{id}/staleness-cause` — staleness explanation, hash details, verification info
5. `GET /api/workflow/{id}/steps` (if workflow/task) — dFlow step data

**Lazy-loaded on tab activation:**
- `GET /api/nodes/{id}/edges` — inbound/outbound edges (fetched on first Relationships tab visit, cached in `_cache.edges`)

**Tab content distribution:**

| Tab | Renderer | Content |
|---|---|---|
| Overview | `_renderOverviewTab()` | Badges, ID+copy, location+copy, tags, level_0, staleness cause block, verify button+reason, verification attribution |
| Docs | `_renderDocsTab()` | level_1 ("Level 1 — Description"), level_2 ("Level 2 — Details"), empty state |
| API | `_renderApiTab()` | Autodoc (params/returns/raises), docstring, merged steps (cortex + dFlow unified via `_mergeSteps()`), "View Source" stub |
| Relationships | `_renderRelationshipsTab()` | Test coverage, inbound/outbound edge tables with jump links (lazy-loaded) |
| History | `_renderHistoryTab()` | Verification record, rename history, full change history |

**Tab bar** is rendered dynamically in `#detail-tabs` (between `#detail-actions` and `#detail-content`). Tab switching calls `_activateTab(key)` which routes to the appropriate renderer, resets scroll position, and wires event listeners via `_wireTabEvents()`.

**Verify flow:** The Overview tab shows a "Mark Verified" button for stale nodes. Clicking expands an inline form with optional reason input. On confirm, POSTs to `/api/nodes/{id}/verify`, re-fetches staleness-cause, updates `App.state.stalenessMap` and Cytoscape node data, then re-renders the Overview tab in-place.

### Diff integration (depends on history VCS change IDs)

**Source panel diff toggle:** A "Diff" button is added to the source panel header (`#list-source-controls`) next to the existing "Focus" button. When active, the source panel swaps from `monaco.editor.create` (read-only viewer) to `monaco.editor.createDiffEditor` (side-by-side diff). Monaco 0.52.0 (already loaded via CDN) includes the diff editor — no additional dependencies.

**Diff data flow:**
1. Source panel calls `GET /api/nodes/{node_id}/diff` (new endpoint)
2. Server finds baseline SHA: most recent history row with `change_type IN ('AGENT_VERIFIED', 'MANUAL_VERIFIED', 'CHECKPOINT')` and non-NULL `git_sha`
3. Server runs `git show {sha}:{file_path}` to retrieve old file content
4. Server slices both old and current content to the node's `level_3_location` line range
5. Returns `{old_content, new_content, baseline_sha, baseline_date}`
6. Frontend passes both strings to `monaco.editor.createDiffEditor` as original/modified models

**History tab clickable SHAs:** Each git SHA in the change history table becomes a clickable link. Clicking a SHA opens the source panel in diff mode showing what the code looked like at that commit vs the previous history row's commit. This lets developers trace individual changes through the timeline.

**Graceful fallback:** If no baseline SHA exists (never verified) or `git show` fails (SHA unreachable after rebase/GC), the Diff button is disabled with tooltip "No baseline available." The source panel continues to work normally in read-only and focus modes.

**Linked nodes:**
- `cortex::cortex.viz.server::get_node_edges` — get_node_edges(node_id) — Return inbound and outbound edges for a node (single hop, no traversal).  @ cortex/viz/server.py#L614-L627
- `cortex::cortex.viz.server::get_staleness_cause` — get_staleness_cause(node_id) — Return a human-readable explanation of why a node is stale (or clean).  @ cortex/viz/server.py#L481-L538
- `cortex::cortex.viz.server::verify_node` — verify_node(node_id, body) — Mark a node as verified.  @ cortex/viz/server.py#L440-L463

sections.heading: Key Interactions
sections.content:
### Open Detail
```
App.selectNode(id):
  Parallel fetch: node, history, tests, staleness-cause, [workflow steps]
  Detail.show(node, history, staleness, wfSteps, tests, stalenessCause):
    Store all data in Detail._* properties
    Reset _cache (lazy data)
    _renderTabs() — build 5 tab buttons in #detail-tabs
    _activateTab(Detail._activeTab) — render active tab content
    _wireTabEvents() — bind copy buttons, verify form handlers
```

### Switch Tab
```
User clicks tab button:
  _activateTab(tabKey):
    Update _activeTab, toggle active class on buttons
    Reset scroll position
    Call tab renderer (overview/docs/api/relationships/history)
    Set #detail-content.innerHTML
    _wireTabEvents() — rebind event listeners
```

### Verify Node
```
User clicks 'Mark Verified' on Overview tab:
  Expand inline form (reason input + Confirm/Cancel)
  User clicks Confirm:
    POST /api/nodes/{id}/verify  body: {reason, verified_by: 'human'}
    Re-fetch /api/nodes/{id}/staleness-cause
    Update App.state.stalenessMap[id] = 'VERIFIED'
    Update Cytoscape node data if graph visible
    Re-render Overview tab in-place
```

### Lazy-load Edges (Relationships Tab)
```
User clicks Relationships tab (first time for this node):
  Render loading placeholder
  _fetchEdges():
    GET /api/nodes/{id}/edges
    Guard: check _nodeId and _activeTab still match (race condition)
    Store in _cache.edges
    Re-call _activateTab('relationships') to render with data
```

### Close Detail
```
User clicks X or presses Escape:
  Detail.hide() — clear #detail-tabs innerHTML, hide panel
  App.state.selectedNodeId = null
  Deselect node in graph/list
```

sections.heading: Implementation Trade-offs
sections.content:
### innerHTML rebuild per tab switch
Each tab activation rebuilds `#detail-content.innerHTML` entirely. Given the small DOM per tab (3-8 sections), this is simpler than diffing. Event listeners are re-bound via `_wireTabEvents()` after each render.

### Tab state persistence
`Detail._activeTab` survives `show()` calls — selecting a different node keeps the same tab active. This avoids the jarring UX of always resetting to Overview when comparing nodes.

### Lazy loading for Relationships tab
Edges are fetched only on first Relationships tab activation (not on node selection). This avoids blocking initial render with an extra API call for a tab the user may never open. A race condition guard checks `_nodeId` and `_activeTab` before rendering the response.

### Merged steps (cortex + dFlow)
`_mergeSteps()` normalizes `step_num` (cortex) and `step_number` (dFlow) into a unified list sorted numerically. When both sources have the same step number, the dFlow version wins (richer data with jump links). Source badges ("cortex" / "merged") indicate provenance.

### Verify inline form pattern
The verify button uses an expand/collapse pattern instead of a modal. Clicking "Mark Verified" reveals a reason input + Confirm/Cancel row. This keeps the interaction lightweight and in-context. On success, the Overview tab re-renders in-place with updated staleness data.

### Parallel data fetching
Five API calls (node, history, tests, staleness-cause, optionally workflow steps) are fired in parallel via `Promise.all`. The staleness-cause fetch uses `.catch(() => null)` so a failure doesn't block the entire panel.

sections.heading: Technical Verification
sections.content:
- Tab bar renders 5 tabs; clicking each shows the correct content
- Tab state persists across node selections within the same session
- Overview: badges, ID, location, tags, summary, staleness cause, verify button all render
- Overview: verify button expands to show reason input; submitting updates staleness badge
- Overview: verification attribution shows who/when/reason after verify
- Docs: level_1 and level_2 show with "Level 1 — Description" / "Level 2 — Details" labels
- Docs: empty state message when no docs available
- API: autodoc params/returns/raises render, docstring shows, merged steps list appears
- API: "View Source" button present (stub for Phase 3)
- Relationships: test coverage table renders with pass/fail icons and jump buttons
- Relationships: edge tables load lazily on first tab visit, show loading state, then render
- Relationships: inbound/outbound edges show with jump links and edge type badges
- History: full change history (no 10-item cap), verification record, rename history sub-section
- Copy buttons copy correct text to clipboard in all tabs
- Jump buttons navigate to connected nodes
- Dark mode: all new elements styled correctly
- Panel closes on X click and Escape key
